reasoning: Ariarne Titmus currently holds the women's 200m freestyle long-course world record (1:52.23, established in June 2024). A 1:52.23 is an exceptionally fast time that usually requires a full taper and optimal meet conditions to challenge. Historically, long-course world records change hands predominately at major international championships or national Olympic/World trials. In 2026, there are no Olympic Games or Long Course World Championships (the Short Course Worlds are set for December 2026). The major focus meets for top competitors—like Mollie O'Callaghan or Summer McIntosh—will be the Pan Pacific Championships and Commonwealth Games, which are scheduled for late summer (July/August). Furthermore, Australian Trials/Championships are expected to fall in April, with the Age Championships scheduled to begin on April 11, pushing major tapered racing to either at or beyond the final April 4 resolution date. Consequently, it is highly unlikely that anyone will break this record in the early-season un-tapered meets (like the Pro Swim Series or state championships) held between January and early April. Given the short time horizons, probability remains extremely high for the January and February dates, tapering down slightly for April in case of an exceptionally fast, fully rested swim at a precursor meet or an unpredictably early trial.
